task_completion.description: Open the target presentation in LibreOffice Impress
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Impress is open and the title bar displays 'classical_mechanics.odp'.

task_completion.description: Insert the 'Newton's Second Law' formula on Slide 2
task_completion.visual_evidence: Slide 2 is selected in the slides pane. A mathematical formula object displaying 'F = m × a' (or 'F = m a') is visible on the slide.

task_completion.description: Insert the 'Kinetic Energy' formula on Slide 3
task_completion.visual_evidence: Slide 3 is selected in the slides pane. A mathematical formula object displaying 'E_k = 1/2 m v^2' (with 1/2 formatted as a fraction) is visible on the slide.

task_completion.description: Insert the 'Mass-Energy Equivalence' formula on Slide 4
task_completion.visual_evidence: Slide 4 is selected in the slides pane. A mathematical formula object displaying 'E = m c^2' is visible on the slide.

task_completion.description: Use embedded Formula objects (OLE) via LibreOffice Math
task_completion.visual_evidence: In at least one screenshot, the LibreOffice Math editor interface must be visible (typically a formula text input box at the bottom of the screen and/or an 'Elements' pane), proving OLE objects were used instead of standard text boxes.
A 'Save' action is performed (e.g., clicking the floppy disk icon, File -> Save) or the final screenshot shows no unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk) in the title bar.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the LibreOffice Impress and Math graphical interfaces, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts modifying the .odp file directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the formulas using plain text boxes
integrity.visual_evidence: The formulas appear as properly formatted mathematical equations (e.g., actual fractions, proper superscripts) and not just raw markup text like '{1} over {2}' typed into a standard Impress text box.

integrity.description: The agent edited the existing presentation rather than creating a new one
integrity.visual_evidence: The presentation contains the original 5 slides with their respective titles (e.g., 'Newton's Second Law', 'Kinetic Energy'), not a blank or newly created file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is /home/ga/Documents/Presentations/classical_mechanics.odp, which contains 5 slides. Slide 2 must contain a formula with variables 'f', 'm', 'a' (F = m times a). Slide 3 must contain a formula with variables 'e', 'm', 'v' (E_k = 1/2 m v^2). Slide 4 must contain a formula with variables 'e', 'm', 'c' (E = m c^2). The formulas must be embedded LibreOffice Math objects (OLE), not standard text boxes.
